title: Critical Language Scholarship – Arabic Program (Meknes, Morocco)
description: A fully funded U.S. Department of State summer program that sends U.S. undergraduate and graduate students to Meknes, Morocco for 8-10 weeks of intensive Arabic language and cultural immersion instruction at the Arab American Language Institute. Approximately 315 students nationwide were selected for the 2026 cohort across all CLS languages and sites. Implemented by American Councils for International Education.
org: Critical Language Scholarship Program (U.S. Department of State / American Councils for International Education)
visaNote: U.S. citizens generally enter Morocco visa-free for stays under 90 days; CLS coordinates any required paperwork with the host institute and homestay families.

title: AIMS Short-Term Research Grant (Tunisia)
description: The American Institute for Maghrib Studies awards research grants of up to $6,500 (1-3 months) or $15,500 (longer projects) to U.S. scholars and graduate students conducting fieldwork in Tunisia, inclusive of airfare to/from the U.S. Grantees are affiliated with AIMS's Tunis-based overseas research center (CEMAT) and must present their research there and submit a completion report.
visaNote: Grantees must arrange their own Tunisian entry/research visa; AIMS's Tunis center (CEMAT) assists with local logistics.
